title_en: An-Nawawi's Linguistic Analysis in the Sharh
content_en:
Arabic language analysis is one of the most prominent features of an-Nawawi's commentary on Sahih Muslim, and it reflects both his personal scholarly formation and his understanding of what students of hadith genuinely need. An-Nawawi studied Arabic grammar deeply under the scholars of Damascus, and the precision of his linguistic explanations in the Sharh goes beyond what was strictly necessary for establishing legal rulings. He treats the language of hadith as worthy of serious philological attention, not merely as a vehicle for transmitting content.

His approach to vocabulary is particularly careful. When a hadith uses a word that carries technical legal meaning, an-Nawawi first establishes the word's root meaning in classical Arabic, then shows how that root meaning relates to the technical usage, and then distinguishes between the various derived meanings that might affect how the hadith is understood. This method ensures that a student understands not just what a word means in a given context but why it means that — a deeper form of understanding that allows for more confident application.

Grammatical analysis appears throughout the commentary, especially when the grammatical structure of a sentence in a hadith affects its meaning in legally or theologically significant ways. The placement of a particle, the case marking of a noun, the tense of a verb — all of these can determine how a command or description in a hadith should be interpreted, and an-Nawawi is attentive to these details in a way that reflects genuine grammatical training rather than superficial annotation.

He is also alert to dialectal variation and the relationship between Classical Arabic and the spoken Arabic of the Prophet's time and region. Some hadith texts contain words or usages that were characteristic of the Hijazi dialect, and an-Nawawi notes these features while explaining their meaning. This attention to historical linguistics, while not as developed as it would become in later centuries, shows an awareness that the Arabic of the Prophet's time was not identical to the standardized literary Arabic of later centuries.

An-Nawawi's treatment of apparent grammatical irregularities in hadith texts is particularly instructive. Sometimes a hadith as transmitted appears to contain a grammatical error — a case ending that seems wrong, a verb form that seems unexpected. An-Nawawi consistently offers explanations that preserve the correctness of the Prophet's speech, either by showing that the apparent irregularity follows a recognized Arabic usage pattern or by noting that the issue lies in the transmission rather than the original utterance.

Beyond grammar and vocabulary, an-Nawawi attends to the rhetorical dimensions of hadith language. The Prophet's speech was famous in the early Muslim community for being comprehensive yet concise — what was described as 'jawami' al-kalim,' the ability to convey much in few words. An-Nawawi regularly points out where a hadith text achieves this compression, showing how a short phrase contains multiple layers of meaning that extended analysis can unpack. This attention to rhetoric elevates the Sharh from a technical commentary to a work that genuinely illuminates the literary quality of the prophetic tradition.
content_id:
Analisis bahasa Arab adalah salah satu fitur paling menonjol dari komentar al-Nawawi atas Sahih Muslim, dan ia mencerminkan baik pembentukan keilmuan pribadinya maupun pemahamannya tentang apa yang benar-benar dibutuhkan oleh para pelajar hadis. Al-Nawawi mempelajari tata bahasa Arab secara mendalam dari para ulama Damaskus, dan ketepatan penjelasan linguistiknya dalam Syarh melampaui apa yang secara ketat diperlukan untuk menetapkan hukum. Beliau memperlakukan bahasa hadis sebagai layak mendapat perhatian filologis yang serius, bukan sekadar sebagai kendaraan untuk menyampaikan konten.

Pendekatannya terhadap kosakata bersifat sistematis. Ketika sebuah kata dalam hadis memiliki makna teknis yang berbeda dari penggunaan umum — atau ketika telah ada perbedaan pendapat di antara ulama bahasa tentang pengertiannya — al-Nawawi mengidentifikasi opsi-opsi yang ada, menimbang bukti di antara keduanya, dan menetapkan mana yang berlaku dalam konteks ini. Ini bukan latihan akademis; makna kata sering menentukan apakah suatu hadis menetapkan hukum wajib atau sekadar sunnah, apakah ia berlaku secara umum atau terbatas pada konteks tertentu.

Analisis gramatikal al-Nawawi adalah fitur paling teknis dari Syarh dan paling sulit bagi pelajar modern tanpa pelatihan tata bahasa Arab klasik yang mendalam. Beliau secara rutin mengidentifikasi fungsi kata-kata dan frasa-frasa tertentu — apakah kata benda tertentu adalah fa'il (subjek) atau maf'ul (objek), apakah klausa tertentu adalah hal (deskripsi keadaan) atau sifah (sifat), apakah penggunaan tertentu dari mudhari' (bentuk kata kerja imperfek) menunjukkan saat ini atau masa depan — karena semua pertanyaan ini mempengaruhi bagaimana hadis dipahami.

Satu contoh ilustratif adalah analisisnya terhadap hadis-hadis yang menggunakan kata la (tidak) dalam konstruksi-konstruksi berbeda. Al-Nawawi secara cermat membedakan antara la yang meniadakan keberadaan, la yang meniadakan kesempurnaan, dan la yang meniadakan validitas hukum — karena ketiga makna ini menghasilkan implikasi yang sangat berbeda. Sebuah hadis yang mengatakan "la salata ila bi-fatihati al-kitab" bisa bermakna bahwa salah tanpa al-Fatihah tidak sah, atau bahwa ia tidak sempurna, tergantung pada bagaimana la dipahami, dan al-Nawawi menunjukkan mengapa satu tafsiran lebih kuat dari yang lain.

Pendekatan al-Nawawi terhadap hadis-hadis yang menggunakan ungkapan kiasan atau majazi juga patut dicatat. Beliau tidak secara otomatis meluruskan kiasan tersebut, tetapi menyelidiki apakah konteksnya mendukung pembacaan harfiah atau majazi dan bagaimana pendapat ulama-ulama terdahulu tentang hal ini. Kesediaannya untuk mempertahankan kekayaan linguistik teks sambil memastikan pemahaman yang tepat mencerminkan pendekatannya yang matang terhadap pengajaran hadis.

Warisan linguistik Syarh ini melampaui analisisnya tentang hadis-hadis individual. Komentar al-Nawawi berfungsi sebagai model untuk cara seorang ulama seharusnya mendekati teks bahasa Arab klasik: dengan pengetahuan mendalam tentang kaidah bahasa Arab, kepekaan terhadap nuansa penggunaan, kesediaan untuk terlibat dengan perbedaan pendapat, dan orientasi yang selalu diarahkan pada pemahaman yang akurat dan penerapan yang benar. Pelajar yang mempelajari Syarh mendapat manfaat tidak hanya dari komentarnya tentang hadis-hadis tertentu tetapi juga dari contoh metodologi bahasa itu sendiri.
content_ar: Arabic language analysis is one of the most prominent features of النووي's commentary on صحيح مسلم, and it reflects both his personal العلمي formation and his الفهم of what students of الحديث genuinely need. النووي studied Arabic grammar deeply under the العلماء of Damascus, and the precision of his linguistic explanations in the Sharh goes beyond what was strictly necessary for establishing legal rulings. He treats the language of الحديث as worthy of serious philological attention, not merely as a vehicle for transmitting content. His approach to vocabulary is particularly careful. عندما a الحديث uses a word that carries technical legal meaning, النووي أولاً establishes the word's root meaning in الكلاسيكي Arabic, then shows how that root meaning relates to the technical usage, and then distinguishes between the various derived meanings that might affect how the الحديث is understood. This method ensures that a student understands not just what a word means in a given context but why it means that — a deeper form of الفهم that allows for more confident application. Grammatical analysis appears throughout the commentary, ولا سيما عندما the grammatical structure of a sentence in a الحديث affects its meaning in legally or theologically المهم ways. The placement of a particle, the case marking of a noun, the tense of a verb — all of these can determine how a command or description in a الحديث should be interpreted, and النووي is attentive to these details in a الطريق that reflects genuine grammatical training rather than superficial annotation. He is أيضاً alert to dialectal variation and the relationship between الكلاسيكي Arabic and the spoken Arabic of النبي ﷺ's time and region. Some الحديث texts contain words or usages that were characteristic of the Hijazi dialect, and النووي notes these features بينما explaining their meaning. This attention to historical linguistics, بينما not as developed as it would become in later centuries, shows an awareness that the Arabic of النبي ﷺ's time was not identical to the standardized literary Arabic of later centuries. النووي's treatment of apparent grammatical irregularities in الحديث texts is particularly instructive. Sometimes a الحديث as transmitted appears to contain a grammatical error — a case ending that seems الخطأ, a verb form that seems unexpected. النووي consistently offers explanations that preserve the correctness of النبي ﷺ's speech, either by showing that the apparent irregularity follows a recognized Arabic usage pattern or by noting that the issue lies in the transmission rather than the original utterance. Beyond grammar and vocabulary, النووي attends to the rhetorical dimensions of الحديث language. النبي ﷺ's speech was famous in the early مسلم الجماعة for being comprehensive yet concise — what was described as 'jawami' al-kalim,' the ability to convey much in few words. النووي regula
